passage_title: Looking for Life on the Ocean Wave

sentences.sentence: Put one buccaneering entrepreneur-cum-bioscientist on a luxury yacht.
sentences.translation: 让一位富有冒险精神、兼具企业家与生物科学家身份的人登上一艘豪华游艇。

structure.text: Put
one buccaneering entrepreneur-cum-bioscientist
on a luxury yacht

difficult_points: 本句是祈使句，省略了主语 you，以 Put 开头营造设想场景。
entrepreneur-cum-bioscientist 中的 cum 表示“兼具……身份”。

sentences.sentence: Using some mighty fine nets, let him trawl the world's oceans for the smallest creatures.
sentences.translation: 让他用一些极细密的网在全球海洋中搜寻最微小的生物。

structure.text: Using some mighty fine nets
let
him trawl the world's oceans
for the smallest creatures

difficult_points: Using 短语说明 trawl 所采用的工具，其逻辑主语是 him。
let him trawl 是 let + 宾语 + 动词原形结构。

sentences.sentence: Catalogue the genetic diversity of this, the most abundant form of life in the largest habitat on Earth.
sentences.translation: 把这种生命形式的遗传多样性编目记录下来——它是地球最大栖息地中数量最丰富的生命形式。

structure.text: Catalogue
the genetic diversity of this
the most abundant form of life in the largest habitat on Earth

difficult_points: this 回指上一句中的 the smallest creatures，即微生物。
逗号后的名词短语是 this 的同位语，对其作补充说明。

expressions.text: the most abundant form of life
expressions.meaning_zh: 数量最丰富的生命形式

sentences.sentence: Then hijack the molecular machinery of these microbes to make clean energy, new drugs or boost the ability of the Earth's lungs to "breathe" more carbon dioxide, and so limit global warming.
sentences.translation: 然后利用这些微生物的分子机制来制造清洁能源和新药，或增强地球“肺部”吸收更多二氧化碳的能力，从而限制全球变暖。

structure.text: Then hijack the molecular machinery of these microbes
to make clean energy, new drugs or boost the ability of the Earth's lungs
to "breathe" more carbon dioxide
and so limit global warming

difficult_points: hijack 在此是比喻，指把微生物的分子机制“拿来为人所用”。
to make 与 boost 并列，表示利用这种机制的两个目的。
the Earth's lungs 比喻能够吸收二氧化碳的海洋生态系统。

sentences.sentence: This may sound like the outline for a sci-fi potboiler, but it sums up the remarkable efforts of Craig Venter, the maverick American scientist.
sentences.translation: 这听起来也许像一部通俗科幻小说的故事梗概，但它概括的却是特立独行的美国科学家克雷格·文特尔所付出的非凡努力。

structure.text: This may sound like the outline for a sci-fi potboiler
but it sums up the remarkable efforts of Craig Venter
the maverick American scientist

difficult_points: This 指代 A 段描述的整套设想。
but 连接听似虚构与实际科研之间的转折。
the maverick American scientist 是 Craig Venter 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: Seven wars ago.
sentences.translation: 七年前。

structure.text: Seven wars ago

difficult_points: 这是源文保留的残句，语义上应与下一句连读。
wars 很可能是 years 的 OCR 错误，正常理解为“七年前”。

sentences.sentence: Venter announced at the White House that he had identified all the genes - the genome - in the DNA of a human being.
sentences.translation: 文特尔在白宫宣布，他已经识别出一个人类 DNA 中的全部基因，也就是整个基因组。

structure.text: Venter announced at the White House
that he had identified all the genes - the genome - in the DNA of a human being
the genome

difficult_points: that 引导 announced 的宾语从句。
had identified 使用过去完成时，表示识别工作发生在宣布之前。
the genome 插入解释 all the genes。

sentences.sentence: It was the culmination of a bitter race with an international consortium of government labs, and his bull-in-a-china-shop approach earned him the epithet "the boy of science".
sentences.translation: 这是他与一个由各国政府实验室组成的国际联盟激烈竞赛的最终成果，而他横冲直撞的做事方式也让他得到了“科学界的男孩”这一称号。

structure.text: It was the culmination of a bitter race
with an international consortium of government labs
and his bull-in-a-china-shop approach earned him the epithet "the boy of science"

difficult_points: It 回指文特尔识别人类全部基因这一成果。
bull-in-a-china-shop 是比喻，形容做事鲁莽、横冲直撞。
earned him the epithet 使用双宾语结构，表示“为他赢得称号”。

sentences.sentence: It did not deter him, and while many of the critics in the scientific establishment who vilified him disappeared from view.
sentences.translation: 这并没有阻止他；与此同时，科学界那些曾诋毁他的批评者中有许多人逐渐淡出了公众视野。

structure.text: It did not deter him
and while many of the critics in the scientific establishment who vilified him disappeared from view
who vilified him

difficult_points: It 回指此前的负面称号和批评。
who vilified him 修饰 critics。
源句中的 while 形式不完整，可按“与此同时”理解并保留原文。

sentences.sentence: Venter went on to become the first person to read his own genome and is also undertaking an extraordinary effort to create a synthetic genome for an artificial organism.
sentences.translation: 文特尔后来成为第一个解读自身基因组的人，并且还在开展一项非凡的工作：为人工生物创造合成基因组。

structure.text: Venter went on to become the first person
to read his own genome
and is also undertaking an extraordinary effort
to create a synthetic genome for an artificial organism

difficult_points: went on to become 表示在此前事件之后“接着成为”。
两个 to 不定式分别修饰 person 和 effort。
is undertaking 强调这项工作当时仍在进行。

sentences.sentence: Today, however, he is bobbing in the middle of the Sea of Cortez, mixing business with pleasure in a project to read marine DNA codes as he sails along the west coast of the Americas.
sentences.translation: 然而如今，他正漂荡在科尔特斯海中央，一边沿美洲西海岸航行，一边在解读海洋 DNA 编码的项目中把工作与乐趣结合起来。

structure.text: Today, however, he is bobbing in the middle of the Sea of Cortez
mixing business with pleasure in a project
to read marine DNA codes
as he sails along the west coast of the Americas

difficult_points: mixing 的逻辑主语是 he，补充说明他航行时所做之事。
to read marine DNA codes 修饰 project。
as 引导时间从句，表示“当……时”。

sentences.sentence: His 29-metre sloop, Sorcerer II, is a floating laboratory.
sentences.translation: 他那艘长 29 米、名为“巫师二号”的单桅帆船就是一座浮动实验室。

structure.text: His 29-metre sloop
Sorcerer II
is a floating laboratory

difficult_points: Sorcerer II 是 sloop 的同位语，即船名。
floating laboratory 是比喻性表语，说明船承担实验室功能。

sentences.sentence: Rather than use the traditional method of studying microorganisms by growing them in the lab, which only works with one species in every 100, Venter is obtaining the genetic codes of anything and everything present in sea water.
sentences.translation: 文特尔没有采用在实验室培养微生物的传统研究方法——这种方法每一百种微生物中只能适用于一种——而是在获取海水中所有生物的遗传编码。

structure.text: Rather than use the traditional method of studying microorganisms by growing them in the lab
which only works with one species in every 100
Venter is obtaining the genetic codes
of anything and everything present in sea water

difficult_points: Rather than 引出被放弃的传统方法，与主句的新方法形成对比。
which 修饰 the traditional method，而非紧邻的 lab。
present in sea water 是过去分词短语，修饰 anything and everything。

sentences.sentence: The result is a radical new view of life in the oceans, the modem answer to Charles Darwin's I 9th-century voyage on the HMS Beagle.
sentences.translation: 其结果是形成了一种看待海洋生命的全新激进视角，堪称对查尔斯·达尔文十九世纪乘“小猎犬号”航行的现代回应。

structure.text: The result
is a radical new view of life in the oceans
the modem answer to Charles Darwin's I 9th-century voyage on the HMS Beagle

difficult_points: 逗号后的名词短语是前面 a radical new view 的同位说明。
modem 和 I 9th-century 是源文 OCR 异常，语义应为 modern 和 19th-century。

sentences.sentence: "We are starting to view the world in a gene-centred fashion," Venter says.
sentences.translation: 文特尔说：“我们开始以基因为中心来看待世界。”

structure.text: We are starting to view the world in a gene-centred fashion
Venter says

difficult_points: 引语置于 reporting clause 之前，但逻辑上是 says 的内容。
in a gene-centred fashion 表示看待世界的方式。

sentences.sentence: "Our goal is to try to sort out evolution, working back from the genes to what organisms are there."
sentences.translation: “我们的目标是尝试弄清进化过程，从基因反向追溯，判断那里存在着哪些生物。”

structure.text: Our goal
is to try to sort out evolution
working back from the genes
to what organisms are there

difficult_points: to try to sort out 连用两个不定式，前者作表语，后者作 try 的宾语。
working back 表示从结果反向推导，其逻辑主语是 we。
what organisms are there 是 what 引导的名词性从句，作介词 to 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: He calls his approach"metagenomics".
sentences.translation: 他把这种方法称为“宏基因组学”。

structure.text: He
calls
his approach
"metagenomics"

difficult_points: call + 宾语 + 名称是宾语补足语结构，表示“把……称为……”。
approach 与引号之间缺少空格是源文排版异常。

sentences.sentence: Microbes make up the vast majority of life on the planet and account for up to 90 per cent of the biological mass in the sea.
sentences.translation: 微生物构成了地球上绝大多数生命，并占海洋生物量的多达 90%。

structure.text: Microbes
make up the vast majority of life on the planet
and account for up to 90 per cent of the biological mass in the sea

difficult_points: make up 与 account for 是并列谓语，都以 Microbes 为主语。
up to 表示最高可达该比例。

expressions.text: make up the vast majority of

sentences.sentence: They are the central processors of matter and energy in ecosystems.
sentences.translation: 它们是生态系统中物质与能量的核心处理者。

structure.text: They
are the central processors
of matter and energy in ecosystems

difficult_points: They 回指上一句的 Microbes。
processors 是拟人化表达，强调微生物在物质和能量循环中的核心作用。

sentences.sentence: They are responsible for the creation and maintenance of the air we breathe.
sentences.translation: 我们呼吸的空气得以产生和维持，都是它们的作用。

structure.text: They
are responsible for the creation and maintenance of the air
we breathe

difficult_points: we breathe 是省略关系代词 that/which 的定语从句，修饰 the air。
creation and maintenance 是并列名词，共同受 for 支配。

sentences.sentence: They are also, perhaps, our biggest hope of slowing global warming.
sentences.translation: 它们或许也是我们减缓全球变暖的最大希望。

structure.text: They
are also, perhaps, our biggest hope
of slowing global warming

difficult_points: perhaps 是插入性副词，表示作者对判断有所保留。
of slowing global warming 修饰 hope，说明希望实现的目标。

sentences.sentence: Our oceans are the biggest"sink" of carbon, thanks in part to organisms that absorb carbon from the atmosphere to build their skeletons and shells, like "lungs".
sentences.translation: 海洋是最大的碳“汇”，这在一定程度上要归功于一些生物：它们像“肺”一样从大气中吸收碳来构建自己的骨骼和外壳。

structure.text: Our oceans are the biggest"sink" of carbon
thanks in part to organisms
that absorb carbon from the atmosphere
to build their skeletons and shells

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 organisms。
to build 表示这些生物吸收碳的目的。
sink 和 lungs 都是比喻，分别突出储碳和吸碳功能。

sentences.sentence: Remarkably, the vast majority of these organisms are unknown.
sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，这些生物中的绝大多数仍不为人知。

structure.text: Remarkably
the vast majority of these organisms
are unknown

difficult_points: Remarkably 修饰整个句子，表达作者评价。
unknown 在此不是“身份不明”，而是尚未被科学认识和描述。

sentences.sentence: "It is important to understand their role and function to ensure the survival of the planet and human life," says Venter, who is founder and chairman of the J. Craig Venter Institute in Rockville, Maryland.
sentences.translation: 文特尔说：“了解它们的作用和功能，对于确保地球及人类生命的存续至关重要。”文特尔是位于马里兰州罗克维尔的 J. Craig Venter 研究所的创始人兼主席。

structure.text: It is important to understand their role and function
to ensure the survival of the planet and human life
says Venter
who is founder and chairman of the J. Craig Venter Institute in Rockville, Maryland

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正主语是 to understand their role and function。
第二个 to 不定式表示了解微生物的目的。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充文特尔的身份。

sentences.sentence: The Sorcerer II expedition began with a pilot project in 2003 in the Sargasso Sea near Bermuda in which more than a million new genes were discovered in what was thought to be the marine equivalent of a desert.
sentences.translation: “巫师二号”考察始于 2003 年在百慕大附近马尾藻海开展的一个试点项目；研究人员在那里发现了一百多万个新基因，而此前人们认为那片海域相当于海洋中的沙漠。

structure.text: The Sorcerer II expedition began with a pilot project in 2003 in the Sargasso Sea near Bermuda
in which more than a million new genes were discovered
in what was thought to be the marine equivalent of a desert

difficult_points: in which 修饰 pilot project，也可理解为“在该项目中”。
what 引导名词性从句作介词 in 的宾语。
was thought to be 是被动结构，表示“此前被认为是”。

sentences.sentence: For the next two years, Venter flew back and forth to join the crew as it sampled the waters from Halifax, Nova Scotia, to the Eastern Tropical Pacific.
sentences.translation: 在接下来的两年里，文特尔反复乘飞机往返加入船员队伍；船队从新斯科舍省哈利法克斯一直到东部热带太平洋采集水样。

structure.text: For the next two years
Venter flew back and forth to join the crew
as it sampled the waters
from Halifax, Nova Scotia, to the Eastern Tropical Pacific

difficult_points: to join 表示文特尔往返飞行的目的。
as 引导时间从句，it 指代 crew。
from ... to ... 表示采样海域的跨度。

sentences.sentence: 'I did all the major ocean passages." he said.
sentences.translation: 他说：“所有主要的远洋航程我都参加了。”

structure.text: I did all the major ocean passages
he said

difficult_points: did 在此不是助动词，而是“完成、经历”的实义动词。
开头单引号、结尾双引号及句号位置不一致，均为源文标点异常。

sentences.sentence: One in particular, through the Panama Canal, up to Cocos Island and down to the Galapagos, "was a transforming event, phenomenal" as he combined genomics with writing an autobiography and diving with sharks, all under the gaze of a Discovery Channel TV crew.
sentences.translation: 其中尤其有一次航程穿过巴拿马运河，北上科科斯岛，再南下加拉帕戈斯群岛；那是“一次带来蜕变的非凡经历”，因为他把基因组学研究、撰写自传和与鲨鱼潜水结合在一起，而这一切都在探索频道摄制组的镜头注视下进行。

structure.text: One in particular, through the Panama Canal, up to Cocos Island and down to the Galapagos
"was a transforming event, phenomenal"
as he combined genomics with writing an autobiography and diving with sharks
all under the gaze of a Discovery Channel TV crew

difficult_points: One 回指前句的 major ocean passages。
as 在此说明这次经历非凡的原因或伴随背景。
writing an autobiography 与 diving with sharks 并列，共同接在 with 后。

sentences.sentence: Using phenomenal computing power to reconstruct and analyse microbial DNA, with a single stage of the calculations taking more than a million hours of supercomputer time, a flood of discoveries has come from the latest phase of the expedition.
sentences.translation: 借助强大的计算能力重建并分析微生物 DNA——其中仅一个计算阶段就耗费了超过一百万小时的超级计算机时间——此次考察的最新阶段涌现出大量发现。

structure.text: Using phenomenal computing power to reconstruct and analyse microbial DNA
with a single stage of the calculations taking more than a million hours of supercomputer time
a flood of discoveries has come from the latest phase of the expedition

difficult_points: with + 名词 + taking 构成独立结构，补充计算量之大。
Using 短语按语义修饰研究团队的工作，但源句主句形式上以 discoveries 为主语，存在悬垂修饰。
a flood of 是比喻，表示发现数量很多。

sentences.sentence: Venter announced in a trio of papers in PloS Biology a few days ago that his team had returned to port with 400 newly discovered microbes and six million new genes.
sentences.translation: 几天前，文特尔在发表于《PloS Biology》的三篇论文中宣布，他的团队返港时带回了 400 种新发现的微生物和 600 万个新基因。

structure.text: Venter announced in a trio of papers in PloS Biology a few days ago
that his team had returned to port
with 400 newly discovered microbes and six million new genes

difficult_points: that 引导 announced 的宾语从句。
had returned 使用过去完成时，表示返港早于宣布。
with 表示团队返港时所带回的科研成果。

sentences.sentence: Each gene contains the instructions used to make the proteins that build and operate living thing'-, and Waiter's bounty doubles the number known to science.
sentences.translation: 每个基因都包含制造蛋白质所需的指令，而这些蛋白质构建并维持生物体的运作；文特尔的这批成果使科学界已知的基因数量翻了一番。

structure.text: Each gene contains the instructions
used to make the proteins
that build and operate living thing'-
and Waiter's bounty doubles the number known to science

difficult_points: used to make 是过去分词短语，修饰 instructions，并非“过去常常”。
that 修饰 proteins，build 与 operate 是并列谓语。
living thing'- 和 Waiter's 是源文 OCR 异常，语义应为 living things 和 Venter's。

sentences.sentence: His company, Synthetic Genomics, wants to harness this genetic information to use the microbes to turn carbon dioxide into propane and other fuels, short-circuiting the traditional geological process where ancient creatures are compressed into coal and oil over the aeons.
sentences.translation: 他的公司 Synthetic Genomics 希望利用这些遗传信息，借助微生物把二氧化碳转化为丙烷及其他燃料，从而绕过古代生物经过漫长年代受压形成煤和石油的传统地质过程。

structure.text: His company, Synthetic Genomics, wants to harness this genetic information
to use the microbes to turn carbon dioxide into propane and other fuels
short-circuiting the traditional geological process
where ancient creatures are compressed into coal and oil over the aeons

difficult_points: 第一个 to 表示利用遗传信息的目的，第二个 to 是 use ... to do 结构。
short-circuiting 表示由前述做法带来的结果，即绕过传统过程。
where 修饰 process，说明该地质过程中发生的事情。

sentences.sentence: Another target is hydrogen production, the ultimate clean fuel.
sentences.translation: 另一个目标是生产氢气——这种终极清洁燃料。

structure.text: Another target
is hydrogen production
the ultimate clean fuel

difficult_points: the ultimate clean fuel 是 hydrogen 的同位说明，而非 production。
Another 承接上一句将二氧化碳转化为燃料的目标。

sentences.sentence: When it conn's to climate change, the expedition has thrown up another key insight.
sentences.translation: 谈到气候变化，这次考察又带来了一项关键认识。

structure.text: When it conn's to climate change
the expedition
has thrown up another key insight

difficult_points: conn's 是源文 OCR 异常，按固定表达 comes to 理解。
throw up 在此不是“呕吐”，而是“产生、带来”。

sentences.sentence: Some parts of the ocean have more carbon-hungry organisms than others, and it used to he thought that populations reflected local nutrient levels.
sentences.translation: 海洋中有些区域的碳吸收型生物比其他区域更多，过去人们认为这些种群数量反映了当地的营养水平。

structure.text: Some parts of the ocean have more carbon-hungry organisms than others
and it used to he thought
that populations reflected local nutrient levels

difficult_points: more ... than others 构成比较，others 指海洋中的其他区域。
it 是形式主语，that 从句是真正被认为的内容。
he 是源文 OCR 错误，应按 be 理解。

expressions.text: it used to be thought that

sentences.sentence: Venter has found that t his may not be the case.The culprit could be bacterial viruses phages - which keep microbe levels low in some seas.
sentences.translation: 文特尔发现情况可能并非如此。罪魁祸首可能是噬菌体，也就是使某些海域微生物数量维持在低水平的细菌病毒。

structure.text: Venter has found
that t his may not be the case
The culprit could be bacterial viruses phages
which keep microbe levels low in some seas

difficult_points: 源文在 case.The 之间漏空格，导致两个语义句连成一个文本单元，必须原样保留。
t his 是 this 的 OCR 断裂，回指前一句的传统看法。
which 修饰 phages，说明噬菌体产生的影响。

sentences.sentence: "If we can understand this relationship more, and find out how to inhibit the viruses, or make the bacteria resistant, we would have a lot more organisms capturing carbon dioxide," says Venter.
sentences.translation: 文特尔说：“如果我们能更深入地理解这种关系，弄清如何抑制病毒或使细菌产生抗性，就会有多得多的生物来捕获二氧化碳。”

structure.text: If we can understand this relationship more, and find out how to inhibit the viruses, or make the bacteria resistant
we would have a lot more organisms capturing carbon dioxide
says Venter

difficult_points: If 从句中 understand、find out 和 make 构成相关联的行动链。
how to inhibit 是“疑问词 + 不定式”结构，作 find out 的宾语。
capturing carbon dioxide 是现在分词短语，修饰 organisms。

sentences.sentence: The biggest impact of his project has been on basic science, overturning many established ideas about the tree of life.
sentences.translation: 他的项目对基础科学产生了最大影响，推翻了许多关于生命之树的既有观念。

structure.text: The biggest impact of his project
has been on basic science
overturning many established ideas about the tree of life

difficult_points: has been 使用现在完成时，强调影响延续到当前。
overturning 表示该影响造成的结果。
the tree of life 是生物进化关系的概念性表达。

sentences.sentence: It used to be thought that the protein pigment in our own eyes that enables us to detect light was rare.
sentences.translation: 过去人们认为，我们眼中使我们能够感知光线的蛋白质色素十分罕见。

structure.text: It used to be thought
that the protein pigment in our own eyes that enables us to detect light was rare
that enables us to detect light

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，第一层 that 从句是真正主语。
第二个 that 修饰 protein pigment。
enable us to detect 是 enable + 宾语 + 不定式结构。

expressions.text: it used to be thought that

sentences.sentence: But Venter's gene trawl reveals that all surface marine organisms make proteorhodopsins that detect coloured light.
sentences.translation: 但文特尔的大规模基因搜寻显示，所有海洋表层生物都会产生能够感知有色光的变形视紫红质。

structure.text: But Venter's gene trawl reveals
that all surface marine organisms make proteorhodopsins
that detect coloured light

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 reveals 的宾语从句。
第二个 that 修饰 proteorhodopsins，并在从句中作主语。
gene trawl 是对大范围搜寻基因的比喻。

sentences.sentence: "They turn out to be one of the most abundant and important, gene families on the planet," he said.
sentences.translation: 他说：“事实证明，它们是地球上数量最丰富、最重要的基因家族之一。”

structure.text: They
turn out to be one of the most abundant and important, gene families on the planet
he said

difficult_points: They 回指上一句中的 proteorhodopsins。
turn out to be 表示研究后发现事实如此。
important 后的逗号属于源文异常，不影响 abundant 和 important 共同修饰 gene families。

sentences.sentence: Blue and green variants are found in different environments - blue light preferred in the open ocean such as the indigo Sargasso Sea and green light along coasts.
sentences.translation: 蓝色和绿色变体分布在不同环境中——开阔海域（如靛蓝色的马尾藻海）偏向蓝光，而沿海区域偏向绿光。

structure.text: Blue and green variants
are found in different environments
blue light preferred in the open ocean such as the indigo Sargasso Sea
and green light along coasts

difficult_points: 破折号后是对 different environments 的具体解释。
blue light preferred 省略了 is，green light along coasts 又省略了 is preferred。
such as 引出 open ocean 的实例。

sentences.sentence: Venter believes these proteins help microbes to use energy from the sun, as plants do, but without photosynthesis.
sentences.translation: 文特尔认为，这些蛋白质帮助微生物像植物一样利用太阳能，但并不通过光合作用。

structure.text: Venter believes
these proteins help microbes to use energy from the sun
as plants do
but without photosynthesis

difficult_points: believes 后省略了引导宾语从句的 that。
as plants do 中 do 替代 use energy from the sun。
but without photosynthesis 说明相似之中存在关键差异。

sentences.sentence: Instead, they use this "light-harvesting" machinery to pump charged atoms in the equivalent of solar batteries.
sentences.translation: 相反，它们利用这种“采光”机制，在类似太阳能电池的结构中泵送带电原子。

structure.text: Instead
they use this "light-harvesting" machinery
to pump charged atoms
in the equivalent of solar batteries

difficult_points: they 回指微生物，而 this machinery 回指前述蛋白质发挥作用的机制。
to pump 表示使用这种机制的目的。
the equivalent of solar batteries 是类比，帮助理解储能方式。

sentences.sentence: The team discovered many new proteins that protect microbes from UV rays and some that are involved in repairing the damage caused by UV.
sentences.translation: 团队发现了许多保护微生物免受紫外线伤害的新蛋白质，以及一些参与修复紫外线所致损伤的蛋白质。

structure.text: The team discovered many new proteins
that protect microbes from UV rays
and some
that are involved in repairing the damage caused by UV

difficult_points: 两个 that 从句分别修饰 many new proteins 和 some。
some 后省略了 proteins，以避免重复。
caused by UV 是过去分词短语，修饰 damage。

sentences.sentence: They were also surprised to discover that many kinds of protein that were thought to be specific to one kingdom of life were more widespread.
sentences.translation: 他们还惊讶地发现，许多原以为只存在于某一个生物界的蛋白质，其分布实际上更加广泛。

structure.text: They were also surprised to discover
that many kinds of protein that were thought to be specific to one kingdom of life were more widespread
that were thought to be specific to one kingdom of life

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 discover 的宾语从句。
第二个 that 修饰 many kinds of protein。
were thought to be 与 were more widespread 分属不同层级，前者在定语从句中，后者是宾语从句谓语。

sentences.sentence: This is only the start.
sentences.translation: 这仅仅是个开始。

structure.text: This
is only the start

difficult_points: This 概括前文已经取得的一系列发现。
only 强调目前成果只是更大研究进程的起点。

sentences.sentence: "It's clear," says Venter, "that we've only begun to scratch the surface of understanding the microbial world."
sentences.translation: 文特尔说：“很明显，我们对微生物世界的认识才刚刚触及表面。”

structure.text: It's clear
says Venter
that we've only begun to scratch the surface of understanding the microbial world

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语，二者被插入的 says Venter 分隔。
have begun 使用现在完成时，强调认识过程刚开始且延续至今。
scratch the surface 是比喻，表示只获得了非常浅显的认识。
